Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Brahma-madva Gaudiya [iskcon]sampradaya...is it a siksa or diksa line?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-11-15 · 4 answers
..How does the disciplic succession actually work....since there appears to be gaps.....or they did not take diksa from their previous guru in the line?
user [38] · 2009-11-15
See intro paragraph to http://veda.harekrsna.cz/encyclopedia/parampara1.htm

more: http://www.veda.harekrsna.cz/encyclopedia/philosophy2.htm#11
user [154] · 2009-11-16
Strictly speaking it is not siksa and not diksa. It is a line of acaryas, or liberated souls - our process is to follow the footsteps of acaryas.

Prabhupada would call it Acarya Parampara.
user [459] · 2009-11-16
Thank you Veda prabhu ...Silver spoons.love the story of the passing of Narrotama das takura,how he was within ganges,sorrounded by devotees chanting hari....and while his devotees massaged him he dissolved into the ganges river,hare krishna ...Transcendental sampradaya....
user [459] · 2009-11-25
Srila Bhaktisiddantas Saraswati'92s disciples have adopted his concept of prioritizing teaching (siksha) over
formal ordination (diksha) as a sign of relationship and a marker of disciplic
succession. They thus wish to establish that the renunciate bhajananandi Jagannath
Das was more significant in Bhaktivinoda Thakur'92s life than Bipin Bihari Goswami,
who represents the Gaudiya Maths b'eate noire'97the householder Vaishnava born in
the traditional guru families....
